nodes.label: Packed lower-triangular storage in array W (H followed by overlap block)
nodes.rationale: W holds H in packed lower-triangular order followed by the overlap/identity matrix at offset NPOINT*(NPOINT+1)/2; IW(I)=I*(I+1)/2 stores diagonal pointers. Python port should use a dense symmetric matrix instead (NPOINT <= 1000).
nodes.norm_label: packed lower-triangular storage in array w (h followed by overlap block)

nodes.label: Spectroscopic constants we, wexe, weye, alfae, gamae (finite differences of first 4 levels)
nodes.rationale: we/wexe/weye are first assigned hard-coded literature values used to compute alfae/gamae (DVR.f:342-353), then recomputed from finite differences of levels W(1..4) (DVR.f:356-364); the recomputed values are what get written to fort.4 and fort.97.
nodes.norm_label: spectroscopic constants we, wexe, weye, alfae, gamae (finite differences of first 4 levels)

nodes.label: Constantes2.txt (missing INCLUDE file: De, c1..c6, R0, r_m, J)
nodes.rationale: Missing from the repo but required by the INCLUDE in FUNCTION V; must be recovered (or asked from the user) before recompiling the Fortran or porting the potential to Python.
nodes.norm_label: constantes2.txt (missing include file: de, c1..c6, r0, r_m, j)

nodes.label: Unit conversions: 219474.631 hartree to cm-1; 1822.88839 amu to electron mass
nodes.norm_label: unit conversions: 219474.631 hartree to cm-1; 1822.88839 amu to electron mass

nodes.label: Optimized DVR / X-matrix pipeline (partially disabled)
nodes.rationale: Partially disabled: the second EIGCLL call that would diagonalize the X matrix is commented out (DVR.f:76), so XVCT is never filled; DVR_OP writes V(dble(I)) using the grid index instead of the coordinate (DVR.f:146-147). CLAUDE.md marks FBR/OPTMZD/DVR_OP as partially commented out.

nodes.label: fort.18 / fort.19 (STLM scratch: KFILE=18 sequential, DAFILE=19 direct-access)
nodes.norm_label: fort.18 / fort.19 (stlm scratch: kfile=18 sequential, dafile=19 direct-access)

nodes.label: Python conversion plan (port physics core, replace STLM with SciPy)
nodes.rationale: Port only the ~500-line physics core to Python; do NOT port the ~6300-line STLM Lanczos library. Build the Hamiltonian as a dense symmetric NumPy matrix (packed triangular storage unnecessary since NPOINT <= 1000). Keep Colbert-Miller kinetic matrix elements exactly as in BUILD. Load potential parameters from a config file replacing Constantes2.txt. Preserve fort.4 output values/units for regression comparison.
nodes.norm_label: python conversion plan (port physics core, replace stlm with scipy)

nodes.label: SciPy eigensolver replacement (eigh subset_by_value / eigsh)
nodes.rationale: scipy.linalg.eigh(H, subset_by_value=(ELOW, EHIGH)) or subset_by_index reproduces STLM's eigenvalue-window behavior; scipy.sparse.linalg.eigsh (Lanczos, same algorithm family as STLM) only if N grows large.

nodes.label: Regression verification against committed fort.4 / fort.97
nodes.rationale: Run the Python port with the same fort.3 input (A=13.04, B=30.20, NPOINT=500, AMASS=218947.07152) and diff eigenvalues against committed fort.4 (first level ~9.7211221 cm-1) and fort.97 (we ~19.5309196 cm-1); energies should match to ~1e-6 cm-1.
nodes.norm_label: regression verification against committed fort.4 / fort.97

nodes.norm_label: file i/o map (fortran unit numbers = fort.n filenames)
